data.title: The U.S. Supreme Court did not rule on the legality challenge to Trump's global tariffs.
data.description: The U.S. Supreme Court did not rule on the challenge to the legitimacy of Trump's global tariffs. (Jinshi)

data.title: Mask Network Takes Charge of Lens Community to Boost Decentralized Social Platforms
data.description: Mask Network has assumed the role of steward for the Lens Community, with the objective of promoting decentralized social platforms. According to NS3.AI, the network plans to utilize Lens's open and permissionless infrastructure to develop user-focused products and support creator economies. Current applications backed by MaskDAO, such as Orb Club and Firefly, are already operational within this ecosystem.

data.title: Elon Musk's Platform X Reveals Feed Ranking Algorithm Amid API Restrictions
data.description: Elon Musk's social media platform, X, has made its feed ranking algorithm open-source, unveiling the core system that forecasts user engagement. According to NS3.AI, this move towards transparency aligns with X's decision to limit API access for applications linked to cryptocurrency engagement, which has led to significant market declines in related tokens. Musk has admitted that the algorithm requires substantial enhancements and intends to provide regular public updates to improve both transparency and performance.

data.title: Cardano Foundation Governance Roadmap First Milestone: Delegating an Additional 220 Million ADA to 11 Community Representatives
data.description: The Cardano Foundation announced on the X platform that it has completed the first milestone of its governance roadmap, delegating approximately 220 million ADA to 11 community representatives (DReps) (approximately 20 million ADA per representative). This delegation focuses on two main areas: adoption and operations, bringing the total amount of ADA delegated to community DReps to 360 million ADA. Simultaneously, the Foundation will self-delegate the remaining approximately 171 million ADA to ensure all assets participate in governance.

data.title: CFTC Chairman Michael Selig launches Future Proof program to upgrade crypto regulation.
data.description: Michael Selig, chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), stated that the agency is developing a new roadmap and announced the launch of a new initiative called Future Proof, aimed at modernizing the way digital assets are regulated. Selig said the derivatives regulator needs an upgrade and plans a broad review of existing rules, aiming to adopt the minimum effective dose of regulation he describes. He pointed out that decades-old rules designed for agricultural futures contracts are not suitable for emerging products or trading venues, and just as U.S. businesses modernize the traditional financial system by leveraging new technologies, the CFTC must upgrade its approach to unleash innovation. Selig, appointed by Donald Trump and sworn in last month, stated that if Congress passes legislation on digital asset market structures, the CFTC will assume broad new responsibilities and ensure these markets thrive domestically through a tailored regulatory framework, maintaining the U.S. market's leading position. Currently, lawmakers are debating a bill to expand the CFTC's jurisdiction over the digital asset industry. (The Block)

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Opens with Declines Amid Tech Sector Challenges
data.description: The U.S. stock market opened with significant declines as major indices experienced a downturn. According to ChainCatcher, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell by 0.72%, the S&P 500 dropped by 1.28%, and the Nasdaq Composite decreased by 1.58%. Nvidia's shares declined by 2.4% following the suspension of key component production for its H200 chips by a supplier. Novo Nordisk saw a 3.4% drop, while Amazon's stock fell by 2.3% amid expectations of continued layoffs in the future.

data.title: Lens Completes Feasibility Phase for Decentralized Social Network
data.description: Lens has announced the completion of its feasibility phase for a user-owned decentralized social network. According to PANews, the focus of the ecosystem's development will now shift from protocol and infrastructure to consumer applications and user adoption. Mask Network is seen as the successor in this new phase, aiming to advance decentralized social networking from technical experimentation to everyday use. Stani and Avara will continue to serve as advisors to Mask founder Suji Yan and his team.

data.title: Bridgewater Associates founder Ray Dalio: Trump's policies could trigger a "capital war".
data.description: Bridgewater Associates founder Ray Dalio stated that Trump's policies could trigger a "capital war" because trade tensions and a rising deficit are eroding confidence in US assets, forcing investors to turn to gold as a hedging tool. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trader Expands Short Positions on Multiple Cryptocurrencies
data.description:
A trader known as '0xD83...Fd7' has increased short positions on HYPE in the last half hour. According to PANews, the trader initially began shorting ETH last Friday and has since expanded to other cryptocurrencies using rolling profits. Currently, the trader holds short positions in BTC, ETH, HYPE, and XMR, with a total value of $261 million.

The trader has leveraged an initial capital of 3 million USDC to reach $11 million through rolling shorts. The positions are as follows:

- 1,640 BTC short position valued at $150 million, with an entry price of $92,120 and a liquidation price of $94,732, showing a floating profit of $1.98 million.

- 31,093 ETH short position valued at $100 million, with an entry price of $3,270 and a liquidation price of $3,269, showing a floating profit of $6.29 million.

- 728,000 HYPE short position valued at $16 million, with an entry price of $21.77 and a liquidation price of $30.1, showing a floating loss of $60,000.

- 824 XMR short position valued at $450,000, with an entry price of $605, showing a floating profit of $40,000.

data.title: Brother Machi reduced his Ethereum long positions in the past hour, currently holding 4,300 ETH, with a floating loss of nearly $800,000.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that the market experienced a short-term decline, and Machi Dage's 25x Ethereum long position reduced its holdings by 3,700 ETH in the past hour. The current position still holds 4,300 ETH, with a floating loss of nearly $800,000 and a liquidation price of $3,034.

data.title: A trader who was rolling over positions increased their short positions in HYPE over the past half hour, bringing the total value of their short positions across four cryptocurrencies to over $260 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, the short trader 0xD83...Fd7 has increased his short position in HYPE in the past half hour. He started by shorting ETH last Friday and then used his profits to increase his short positions in other cryptocurrencies. He now holds short positions in four cryptocurrencies: BTC, ETH, HYPE, and XMR, with a total value of $261 million. He turned his initial $3 million into $11 million through shorting: shorting 1640 BTC ($150 million), opening at $92,120, liquidating at $94,732, with a profit of $1.98 million; and shorting 31,093 ETH ($100 million), opening at $3,270, liquidating at $3,269, with a profit of $6.29 million. Shorted 728,000 HYPE tokens ($16 million), opening price $21.77, liquidation price $30.1. Unrealized loss of $60,000. Shorted 824 XMR tokens ($450,000), opening price $605, unrealized profit of $40,000.

data.title: Tharimmune Announces $55 Million Stock Offering to Expand Digital Asset Strategy
data.description: Tharimmune, a Nasdaq-listed company, has announced plans to raise $55 million through a registered stock offering. According to Odaily, the company will issue 1,800,000 shares of common stock at a price of $2.92 per share, along with prepaid warrants. Each warrant allows the purchase of up to 17,000,000 shares of common stock at a price of $2.9199 per warrant. The fundraising transaction is expected to be completed by January 21. The new funds will be used to further develop its digital asset management strategy centered around Canton Network tokens.

data.title: Tether has minted 2 billion USDT on the Tron network so far this year.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Tether has just minted an additional 1 billion USDT on the Tron network, bringing the total number of USDT minted on the Tron network this year to 2 billion.

data.title: Spot gold broke through $4,750 per ounce, setting a new all-time high.
data.description: Spot gold surged, breaking through $4,750 per ounce and setting a new all-time high. (Golden10)

data.title: Crypto stocks opened lower across the board, with Circle down 3.83%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the opening of the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average opened down 0.72%, the S&P 500 opened down 1.28%, and the Nasdaq Composite opened down 1.58%. Cryptocurrency stocks generally declined, with BitMine down 7.67%, Circle down 3.83%, Coinbase down 3.18%, and Strategy down 5.12%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stocks and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Glassnode: Institutional investors are accumulating again during Bitcoin consolidation, and the market structure is gradually improving.
data.description: Glassnode's weekly market analysis report released on Monday showed a significant reversal in US spot Bitcoin ETF flows, with strong inflows indicating institutional investors are rebuilding their BTC holdings. While ETF trading volume has increased, the risk of profit-taking remains a concern. Bitcoin has retreated from its recent high of $98,000 to around $90,000. Although the Relative Strength Index (RSI) declined slightly on January 14th, it remains above neutral, indicating the market is in a consolidation phase rather than a trend deterioration. Spot trading volume has increased moderately, with the net buy-sell imbalance exceeding the statistical upper limit, reflecting a significant easing of selling pressure, but demand remains cautious. Futures open interest has increased slightly, indicating speculators are cautiously rebuilding positions; a sharp drop in funding rates suggests reduced urgency for long positions. The options market still prices high uncertainty, and downside protection remains in demand. On-chain activity is stabilizing, with improved active addresses and transaction volume, and a slight increase in network fees. High supply from short-term holders makes prices sensitive to volatility. Overall, Bitcoin is currently in a consolidation phase, but buying pressure is increasing, and institutional interest is returning, causing the market structure to evolve in a more constructive direction.
